He said you know this is neat stuff We could sell this and I looked at him like yeah, you're nuts a series of tests revealed that the goo they created was Amazingly shock-absorbent now they had a solution But they needed to find the most common problem it addressed whether you're a waitress a Stewardess or a construction worker you're standing on your feet 24-7 So we picked a product that we could make a big dent in American society and really help the working blue collar guy and Make a product that's better than anything on the market This Gel and put it inside of a shoe insert it takes out all the shock and energy and impact out of your leg It's mother nature approved and it's safe on your colored fabrics. And so back then In houdini's time people felt really really trapped and that they couldn't get out and that they couldn't escape from their circumstances and so he was using that as a metaphor which made him actually even More popular and more powerful because of the symbolism and the metaphor. So it's 2 million isn't it? Is that the number? So it's over 2 million People have had in this have had Invisalign so far the invisible braces. So that's a big number And it'll make a really cool graphic in the video because we're going to have first one little photo Of a happy smiling person and then we're going to have them pop up We'll never get to 2 million, but we're going to have a crapload of them pop up and fill the screen and isn't that wonderful and but What does that really mean? first of all, there's 250 million people in the country, so I don't know if 2 million is that big of a number or not, but in isolation. It sounds like a big number It doesn't tell us how many people still chose to have traditional braces I don't even know that number. Maybe it's 8 million damn if I know Right, but I know the 2 million sounds like a big number there's uh so This a survey is done by skeptic magazine Of 1,000 people who believe in UFOs and alien abductions If you don't know what that is that's when the alien takes you and takes you up to the thing and probes you and whatever and brings you back There's a whole speaking circuit of this by the way There is there's people who make a living going around to events of believers in UFOs and alien abductions speaking about having been abducted and they've written books and they got dvds and some million subcultures, man So of the thousand people who believe in both UFOs and alien abductions Self-identified Ask for the reasons that they believe 816 of the thousand stated as their number one reason the sheer number of reports means there must be some truth to it So if enough people believe something or are presented as believing something It there must be something to it. It must be true. And yet we know Crowds are you know stupid I mean, you know herd movement is made up entirely of dumb animals That's what it is. But this is a persuasive thing the other number. So here's a Investors business daily survey of A thousand active investors So these are people who are actively in the market 87 percent agreed with this statement If stock prices are rising they are very likely to continue rising 87 percent Of active investors And pretty much people respond to that way to all trends whatever is happening That you can demonstrate is happening people believe Is going to continue to happen We all know that's What goes up must inevitably come down, right? But all all investor balloons are caused this way and they are caused again and again and again so you guys from London right now, you know, you have this giant real estate bubble over there Because of chinese and russian people trying to park their money somewhere. We don't know does it make any money? We don't know Will it ever make any money? We don't know but it's worth eight billion eight billion dollars You know, I mean it's insane, but well those prices are going up So I got to get in because they'll continue to go up, right? So when you understand how people react to these numerical facts You can find some to use for whatever it is that you do that make your case Last there's what I call a stretch number Which is a number that is accurate it is factual About a certain thing and you stretch it over like a rubber band To apply to what it is that you do Right So I often use for a variety of purposes, which is the point I use the Forbes 500 list of the richest People in America which Forbes put out puts out issue out every year if you don't know And there are little profiles of the 500 richest people in the world. Excuse me Well, you have 500 Really rich people right Truth of the matter is most of them own some of everything Because they're really really really rich So every time I've written copy for get rich in real estate guys I've used the fact That over 450 of the 500 richest people in the world are real estate investors Now The implication of that Is that they got to be one of the 500 richest people in the world by investing in real? I didn't say that but that's What happened Inside somebody's head Oh, if I want to be rich I must invest in real estate And I further stretched it because There's nothing in the profile to indicate That sam walton's widow Is necessarily a real estate investor. I have no idea for all I know she rents an apartment But walmart owns real estate And she owns walmart stock. She's a real estate investor as far as I'm concerned But if I want to do it for stock trading or I want to do it for commodities I could use the same list Even if you wanted to do I've never tried to do it, but if you want to do collector cars I'm going to bet you if you facebook and google those 500 people You're going to find a huge number of them own some collector cars They may not be trading in them, but I'll bet you they got them I've not been asked to so there's a lot of this kind of proof and there's the The the weasel language number two so uh, arizona state university. It's either arizona state university or university of arizona That's one or the other ASU joke How do you get a co-ed into your car? Slow down So so it's either ASU. tells me They got hired stats probably pretty low Or they'd be using that stat So they're using the stat that is most Favorable the minute people hear what appears to be a big number Two million Doesn't matter the context 85 percent doesn't matter the context All right, the minute people hear what see feels to them to be a big number They believe a thing is more real than if they hear a small number or no number at all So when you think about how to numerically and statistically express Whatever it is that you do whatever it is that you're selling whatever you're trying to convince somebody of You want so look at the arguments bernie sanders has been making So he's losing every which way you can lose Right, you can't there's no way you so Total vote count losing Right total number of delegates losing Total number of states losing What's his argument momentum? Right, so he's like a stock picker guy in this window of time I've gained x Therefore and it's a big number right so big numbers affect people out of context And you want to look for the big number way To express What it is that you do and if they hear like five or six big numbers boom boom boom boom They're gone They just assume You're it So we are so proactive is Has been for a decade now the number one Selling acne brand in america Now what does that mean? Well It actually doesn't mean much It means My friends at gutty rinker are pretty good at selling acne go up Doesn't really speak to the quality of the glop or the effectiveness of the glop It actually speaks to the effectiveness of the people selling the glop A meaningful stat would be what percentage reordered That would actually tell you something about the glop And by the way And a real stat would tell you something how many people have stayed on it for six months or longer That would really tell you something There's a reason by the way you never see those stats used in any advertising for proactive But leading brand next thing about leading brand if you put it in context To know what that means you would have to know how many brands there are dividing up the market Because just as was the case with trump if there hadn't been 16 other candidates This primary process would have looked very different than it looked with 16 other candidates In a two brand head-to-head, I don't know that proactive would win In a three active head-to-head, I don't know that they would win But up against 300 and some odd brands dividing up the
